questions.text: Which is the exclusive property of any algorithm? Why is it regarded so?
How the tight bound time complexity of any algorithm is defined? Is it applicable for all algorithms? Justify.

Assume a stack of integers is implemented in an array of integers named iStack. The top of the stack is stored in iStack[0]. Write the necessary lines of statements to find whether the stack is empty or not and push an integer onto the stack.

A large integer is in a linked list with one digit per node and the least significant digit occupies the first position in the linked list. Write a function that increments the integer represented in the linked list.
A tree is said to be skewed if no node has left or no node has right. Write the necessary code segment to check whether the given tree is skewed or not.
Name any two data structures used for representing graphs. What are their respective advantages?

Write the recurrence relations representing the time complexities of conventional matrix multiplication and Strassen's matrix multiplication of matrices of order nxn using divide and conquer strategy. How much of overhead is added to Strassen's algorithm while saving one multiplication?
Differentiate Greedy approach from Dynamic programming for algorithm designs.
What is memoization? Where is it used? What it contributes?
State the reasons for why and when Quick sort performs better than merge sort.
Analyze the following code segment and derive its worst case and best case time complexities with appropriate justification. [Insertion sort code segment]
Write the necessary functions to insert, delete and search an element in a circular queue whose implementation is in an array of size N.

A list of values is said to be palindrome list if the respective values from first to last and last to first are same. Write a function that checks whether the given linked list is palindrome list or not. Does your algorithm incur space overhead and if so, how much?

Write a function that finds the smallest and largest values from the given linked list and place them in the first and last nodes respectively using minimum number of interchanges. What is the time and space complexity of your method? Is it optimal? Justify.

Construct a binary search tree from the list of values (23, 40, 70, 12, 50, 19, 15). Convert the resulting tree into a threaded binary tree by replacing the NULL links. Write a non-recursive routine that will return the inorder successor of the given value.

For the graph G with 8 vertices and 13 edges, construct the spanning tree by using depth first traversal starting from vertex v1. Also find the minimum cost spanning tree using Prim's algorithm with starting vertex v1.

An instance of a Knapsack problem is given as [P = (P1, P2, P3, ..., Pn), W = (w1, w2, w3, ..., wn), n, m], where P has the profits, W has the weights, n is the problem size and m is the Knapsack size. Define a greedy strategy which is expected to give maximum profit. Write an algorithm for your greedy strategy.

Consider the list P0..4 = (15, 5, 10, 20, 25) denoting the dimensions of 4 matrices. Compute the optimal order of the matrix multiplications and the number of scalar multiplications required for the optimal order using matrix chain multiplication.

X = <x1, x2, x3, ..., xm> and Y = <y1, y2, y3, ..., yn> are any two sequences of values. Define the optimal sub structure for finding the longest common sub sequence from X and Y. Design an algorithm to compute only the length of the longest common sub sequence using top-down approach with memoization.

Answer true or false: (a) One can implement a stack based on a linked list so that each individual push/pop operation time is O(1). (b) The core data structure of Depth-First Search is a queue. (c) It is possible to append two linked lists in time O(1). (d) Adding an element to a heap has worst-case time complexity O(log n).

You have an array with 10,00,000 distinct elements in random order. You have to search the array 100 times. What will result in less work? Sorting the array with merge sort and then doing the searches using binary search OR just doing the searches with linear search (without sorting)? Justify your answer with calculations.
One can convert an algorithm to a program, but the reverse is not always true. Why?
What is the asymptotic time-complexity of the following pseudo-code fragment in terms of n? for i = 1 to n do; for j = i to n do; for k = 1 to 3 do; Count++;
What characteristics of a problem would suggest that backtracking is a good approach to solve that problem?
How many links have to be updated to insert a node in a doubly linked list. Which are they?
Find the post-order sequence for the given tree.

Suppose you have a hash table implemented using linear probing. The hash function is the identity function h(z) = z. In which order could the elements have been added to the hash table? Given choices A through D, find all correct answers.
State principle of optimality.
When do you say a problem is NP-hard?
Describe a linear time algorithm to find the ith smallest item from a set of n values. Prove that algorithm takes only linear time.
A bank has 15 million Euro, which can be invested into stocks of four companies to get more profits. Solve the problem with a branch and bound algorithm. Note that partial investment is not possible.

The n-queens problem is the problem of determining the number of ways that n queens can be placed on an n x n chess board so that no queen threatens any other. Device a backtracking algorithm that solves the n-queens problem.

Five products are to be manufactured weekly on the same machine from Monday to Friday. Use a branch and bound algorithm technique to find what order the machine should switch between products, and find the minimum switch cost for a week.

There are 8 cities on one side of a river and 9 cities on the other side. Bridges can only be built between cities with the same number. Determine the maximum number of bridges that can be built without any crossing. Design an algorithm using LCS approach.
A student claimed she has proved P = NP by converting 3-SAT to DNF-SAT in polynomial time. What is wrong in the proof?

In the knapsack problem, we have a knapsack of volume V and a collection of n objects. Provide a greedy based algorithm to get maximum profit, and prove that this algorithm always yields the optimal solution.
Provide the structural definition for Queue, and implement same using arrays and linked lists.
Illustrate, algorithmically, how to insert a node as a right child of its parent in inorder threaded binary tree.
In what order does Prim's algorithm add edges to the MST? For the given graph with vertices A through H, find the MST.
Explain KMP string matching algorithm for finding a pattern on a text, and analyze the algorithm.
Prove that the Hamiltonian cycle problem is in NP-Complete.

At the annual meeting of the knights, King Arthur wishes to exile a small set of knights so that no duelling pair remains. Merlin wants to find the smallest such set. Explain why she cannot find an efficient algorithm. How did Merlin model the problem? What problem is Merlin trying to solve (Minimum Vertex Cover / NP-Complete)?

Rithika has a string S consisting of characters from 1 to 9. For every index i, count the number of even numbered characters from i to the end of the string. Given input '574674546476', output should be '7 7 7 6 5 5 4 4 3 2 1 1'.
Define data abstraction with an example.
State why queue is termed as linear data structure?
What is the minimum and maximum number of nodes in a binary tree of height k?
For what type of problems divide and conquer strategy is not suitable? Give an example.
How many different spanning trees can be formed for a complete graph with 5 nodes?
Differentiate Divide and conquer and Dynamic programming paradigms.
State the role of bounding function in backtracking strategy for solving problems.
Define heap stating its properties.
What is the time complexity of Naive string matching algorithm?
Characterize NP problems.

Define the asymptotic notations corresponding to best case, worst case and average case analysis of an algorithm. Write the following equation using the three asymptotic notations: F(n) = 100n^2 + log n.
Write an algorithm to insert a node X after a given node Y in a doubly linked list having a pointer p to the first node.
Write the algorithm to convert infix to postfix expression using stack and illustrate with an example.
Write recursive algorithms to perform in-order and preorder traversal on a binary tree. Perform in-order and preorder traversal on the given tree and output the sequence.
Write the algorithm to choose the kth smallest element from a list of n elements. Analyse the time complexity for the same.
Solve the Knapsack problem using Greedy strategy. Knapsack capacity: 50, Weight of objects: {20, 30, 10, 40}, Corresponding Profit earned: {100, 80, 110, 95}.
Determine the longest common subsequence between the given sequences A and B using Dynamic programming approach: A: 'acdbce', B: 'abcbfe'.
Compare and contrast Backtracking and Branch and bound methods for solving optimization problems. Draw and explain the state space tree structure involved.
Sort the following list using Heap sort in ascending order. Show the heap at each step of processing: 9, 2, 5, 10, 7, 3, 8, 4, 1, 6.
When will collision occur during hashing? Explain any one collision resolution method in detail.
Explain KMP string matching algorithm in brief.
Differentiate between NP Hard and NP complete problems. Explain theory of reducibility.
Solve the recurrence relation using substitution method and verify using Master's method: T(n) = 4T(n/2) + n, n > 1 and T(n) = 1, n = 1.
Construct the MST for the given graph using Kruskal's method explaining the method.
